mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns exceed zero in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and economic momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved corporate output.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 290 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is nullified when GDPC1 growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals supply chain disruptions and weakening economic activity, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is held constant.
